_meta.description: PRC's targeted import bans (and partial lifts) on Taiwanese goods. Direction is implicit: all entries are direction='prc_imports_from_tw'. Status defaults to 'banned'; entries that have been selectively re-opened via PRC-mediated political channels carry status='partial_lift' with effective_date set to the lift date. Compiled from GACC announcements and contemporary news reporting; HS codes are best-effort 8-digit (PRC system). Many actual GACC actions are at the exporter-registration level rather than HS-code level — that nuance lives in the notes field.
_meta.review_cadence: Update when news scrape surfaces new GACC import suspensions or politically-brokered lifts

bans.notes: GACC cited pest (mealybug) contamination. 91% of TW pineapple exports went to PRC pre-ban. Widely framed as political coercion.
97% of TW sugar apple exports went to PRC pre-ban. GACC cited mealybug.
98% of TW wax apple exports went to PRC pre-ban. Same GACC notice as sugar apples.

Banned by GACC 2022-06-13 (oxytetracycline residue + prohibited drug use; 90% of TW grouper exports went to PRC pre-ban). Partially lifted 2023-12-22 — Beijing's TAO approved 6 farms + 1 company under selective audit-and-registration, framed as the outcome of KMT Deputy Policy Director Su Ching-chuan's mediation. Timing: 22 days before TW presidential election. TW Agriculture Ministry warned against politicising fishermen via selective approval. Cheng Li-wun's April 2026 Beijing visit produced further TAO signalling about broader resumption, but no GACC implementation yet.
Same lift as live grouper — selective approval of 6 farms + 1 company.

Suspended alongside other agri products in the August 2022 post-Pelosi crackdown via the GACC registration-suspension mechanism. April 2026: TAO included pomelos among 10 'Taiwan-friendly measures' announced after KMT chair Cheng Li-wun's meeting with Xi Jinping — but TW Agriculture Ministry has not received GACC notification, so this remains a diplomatic signal rather than an implemented lift.
Post-Pelosi visit retaliation. GACC suspended imports of fresh citrus from Taiwan citing pest/pathogen detection.
Post-Pelosi visit retaliation. Suspended alongside chilled white scallops and citrus fruits.

bans.product_zh: 糕餅及焙烤食品（特定廠商）
bans.notes: Exporter-level suspension: GACC suspended import registrations from 35 named Taiwanese biscuit/pastry exporters during the August 2022 post-Pelosi crackdown. The broader registration-suspension action was much wider — a direct CIFER query on 2026-05-21 returned 1,291 Taiwan exporter companies still marked 暂停进口 against 1,046 marked 有效 (~55% suspended). These are company-level registrations; the older '~2,000 products' figure from 2022 reporting referred to product-level SKUs, which is a different unit. Most carried 5-year Decree 248 terms expiring 2026-01-01 — CIFER still surfaces the suspended set in that status post-cliff rather than as lapsed, suggesting the suspensions have hardened into a quasi-permanent state. Queryable at ciferquery.singlewindow.cn under the 港澳台 tab (PRC schema files Taiwan there, not under 境外/foreign).
